results.justification: Pycnocline source page; no LLM mechanism.
Progressive gradient flow for sparsity training adjusts gradient magnitudes but not via interfacial dissipation.
Theoretical convergence analysis; descriptive not prescriptive.
Layer freezing as barrier against destructive gradient flow — same functional effect (block gradients from reaching protected layers).
Selective freezing prevents destructive gradient overwriting low-level features — equivalent end-state to deepest-layer sill-stop.
GradES strategically freezes parameters by gradient convergence — gradient-aware barrier.
SmartFRZ attention-based layer-freezing framework — equivalent layer-group gradient barrier.
Peri-LN normalizes input/output at each sub-layer boundary — constrains residual spikes between layers; same artefact (inter-layer boundary) same effect (constrain gradient magnitude at boundary).
